description: Operational runbook catalog for GrantLayer incident response. This catalog is a design-time artifact; no operational automation or incident response implementation is provided by GL-073.

runbookCategories.description: The API returns no response, 503 errors, or connection refused for all endpoints.
primarySignals: Zero successful requests over a sustained window
firstChecks: Verify runtime mode and deployment version
Check database connectivity and connection pool status
Check secret source connectivity
Review recent deployment or configuration changes
Inspect process logs for startup or crash loops
containmentGuidance: Do not restart blindly without checking logs
If a deployment change triggered the outage, consider rollback before restart
Isolate the affected instance if multiple instances exist
Preserve current logs for post-incident analysis
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Incident Commander if outage persists beyond 5 minutes
Escalate to Database / Persistence SME if database connectivity is the root cause
Escalate to Security Officer if the outage follows a suspected security event
unsafeActions: Restarting without log review
Deleting database files to force recovery
Disabling authentication to restore access
Restoring from an unverified backup snapshot

runbookCategories.description: The API responds but with elevated latency, elevated error rate, partial feature failure, or inconsistent responses.
primarySignals: Partial endpoint failure while others succeed
Inconsistent response content for identical requests
firstChecks: Identify the degraded endpoint or subsystem
Correlate with recent deployments or config changes
Check resource utilization (CPU, memory, connections)
Review error logs for recurring exception patterns
Validate that runtime mode matches deployment environment
containmentGuidance: Consider disabling the degraded endpoint if graceful degradation is supported
Reduce traffic to the affected subsystem if load balancing is available
Do not restart the entire service unless the degraded subsystem cannot be isolated
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Incident Commander if degradation affects >50% of requests
Escalate to Database / Persistence SME if query latency is the root cause
Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer if inconsistent responses involve audit or evidence data
unsafeActions: Restarting the entire service without isolating the degraded component
Suppressing error logs to reduce noise
Bypassing validation logic to force responses

runbookCategories.description: Users or operators cannot authenticate, tokens are rejected unexpectedly, or permissions are incorrectly granted or denied.
primarySignals: Unexpected elevation of access for restricted roles
firstChecks: Distinguish auth provider failure from GrantLayer permission logic failure
Check token validity, expiry, and issuer
Verify runtime mode and whether demo tokens are active
Review recent auth provider or configuration changes
containmentGuidance: Do not disable authentication as a workaround
If a token type is compromised, revoke that token type scope-wide
Preserve auth logs for forensic analysis
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Security Officer for any suspected token compromise or auth bypass
Escalate to Incident Commander if the auth system is unavailable system-wide
unsafeActions: Disabling authentication to restore access
Elevating a demo token to bypass auth failure
Logging raw tokens or passwords during investigation
Sharing token values in communication channels

runbookCategories.description: Any suspicion that a secret (API key, signing key, token, password, connection string) has been exposed, leaked, or compromised.
primarySignals: Secret found in public repository or communication channel
Unauthorized access using a valid credential
firstChecks: Identify which secret is suspected and its scope of usage
Determine whether the secret was logged, transmitted, or stored in plain text
Verify that no raw secrets appear in current logs or incident artifacts
Check recent access logs for anomalous usage
containmentGuidance: Revoke or rotate the suspected secret immediately
Isolate any system or service that received the exposed secret
Do not log the secret value in any incident record or runbook output
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Security Officer immediately
Escalate to Incident Commander if the exposure affects production or multiple tenants
Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer if regulatory notification may be required
unsafeActions: Logging raw secret values during investigation
Delaying rotation to preserve service continuity
Sharing secret values in chat, email, or tickets
Restoring secrets from backup instead of rotating

runbookCategories.description: Database connection failures, query timeouts, data corruption suspicion, or replication lag.
primarySignals: Data corruption suspicion or checksum mismatch
firstChecks: Check database server health independently from application health
Verify connection string and credential validity
Review recent schema migrations or DDL changes
Check disk space, memory, and CPU on the database host
Validate that runtime mode matches the configured persistence backend
containmentGuidance: Reduce application load if connection pool is exhausted
Do not run destructive DDL or DML without a pre-change snapshot
Pause any background jobs that write to the affected database
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Database / Persistence SME for any corruption suspicion or restore need
Escalate to Incident Commander if the issue affects all product records
Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer if audit or provenance records are at risk
unsafeActions: Running unverified DDL or DML on production data
Restoring from backup without validating snapshot integrity
Overwriting audit or provenance records during recovery
Deleting database files to force a restart

runbookCategories.description: Evidence fails verification, hash mismatch, tamper detection triggers, or completeness score anomalies.
firstChecks: Isolate the affected evidence bundle by identifier
Distinguish between payload storage issue and verification logic issue
Check whether the evidence metadata matches the payload reference
Review recent changes to the evidence verification module
containmentGuidance: Preserve the current state of the affected evidence bundle for forensic analysis
Do not delete or modify evidence metadata to force a passing verification
Quarantine the affected bundle if quarantine capability exists
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer for any tamper detection or integrity concern
Escalate to Security Officer if the issue may indicate deliberate tampering
Escalate to Incident Commander if the issue affects a large subset of records
unsafeActions: Deleting evidence metadata to clear a verification failure
Suppressing tamper detection alerts
Recomputing hashes without investigating the root cause
Modifying evidence records to force a passing score

runbookCategories.description: Any suspicion that audit records or provenance records have been altered, deleted, or suppressed; or that the cryptographic integrity chain is broken.
primarySignals: Audit record count or sequence anomaly
Missing audit events for known actions
Audit log write failure or disk-full condition
firstChecks: Freeze any deletion or purge jobs immediately
Run hash chain verification if tooling is available
Compare current audit records with immutable storage copies if available
Review recent operator actions for any that touched audit or provenance data
containmentGuidance: Stop any process that writes, deletes, or modifies audit or provenance records
Preserve the current database state for forensic analysis
Do not attempt to rebuild or recompute audit records without explicit approval
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer immediately
Escalate to Security Officer if tampering is suspected
Escalate to Incident Commander if the integrity concern is systemic
unsafeActions: Overwriting or regenerating audit records
Deleting provenance events to resolve a chain break
Suppressing audit write failures
Modifying timestamps or actor IDs in audit records

runbookCategories.description: Backup failure, backup integrity suspicion, restore need, or recovery point objective violation.
primarySignals: Recovery point objective exceeded (no recent successful backup)
Restore request due to data loss or corruption
firstChecks: Verify the backup scope and whether it includes the required data categories
Check backup integrity checksums or signatures
Identify the most recent successful backup snapshot
Confirm that secrets were excluded from the backup as required
containmentGuidance: Do not improvise destructive recovery steps
Scope the restore to the affected data categories only
Validate the restore snapshot in a non-production environment first if possible
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Database / Persistence SME for any restore that touches product, audit, or provenance data
Escalate to Incident Commander if the RPO violation affects production
Escalate to Audit / Compliance Officer if audit or provenance data is involved in the restore
unsafeActions: Restoring from an unverified backup snapshot
Performing a blanket database dump restore without category scoping
Overwriting audit or provenance records during restore
Restoring secrets from backup instead of using secret-management procedures

runbookCategories.description: The system is running with the wrong runtime mode, unvalidated configuration, or a configuration value that contradicts the deployment environment.
primarySignals: Runtime mode declaration does not match deployment environment
Configuration validation failure at startup or runtime
firstChecks: Identify the declared runtime mode and the expected runtime mode
Review configuration source and recent changes
Check for environment variable overrides or stale config files
Verify that secrets and connection strings match the target environment
containmentGuidance: If the mismatch poses a security or data integrity risk, stop the service until config is corrected
Rebuild configuration from validated deployment artifacts rather than editing ad-hoc
Do not continue running production workloads with demo or test configuration
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Incident Commander if the mismatch affects production
Escalate to Security Officer if the mismatch exposes secrets or disables security controls
Escalate to Database / Persistence SME if the mismatch points to the wrong database
unsafeActions: Continuing to run with a known configuration mismatch
Applying ad-hoc configuration edits without validation
Copying configuration files from one environment to another without review
Disabling configuration validation to allow startup

runbookCategories.description: A deployment has introduced regression, instability, or security exposure and must be rolled back.
primarySignals: Error rate spike immediately after deployment
Newly introduced latency or resource exhaustion
firstChecks: Identify the last known good deployment version
Verify rollback artifact integrity and availability
Review deployment logs and change summary
Confirm that the rollback does not reintroduce a previously fixed vulnerability
containmentGuidance: If the deployment introduced a security exposure, consider stopping traffic before rollback
Preserve the current deployment state and logs for root cause analysis
Do not rollback without confirming the rollback artifact is intact
escalationGuidance: Escalate to Incident Commander if the deployment affects production
Escalate to Security Officer if the deployment introduced a security exposure
Escalate to Database / Persistence SME if the deployment included schema or migration changes
unsafeActions: Rolling back without validating the rollback artifact
Rolling back a database migration without a tested rollback script
Suppressing deployment failure logs
Re-deploying the same failing artifact without root cause analysis
